chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ الْوَقْفِ وَ الصَّدَقَةِ وَ النُّحْلِ وَ الْهِبَةِ وَ السُّكْنَى وَ الْعُمْرَى وَ الرُّقْبَى وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ عَلَى الْوَلَدِ وَ غَيْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – What is allowed from the endowment, and the charity, and the grant, and the gift, and the (provision) of habitation, and the lifetime (service) and the bondman, and what is allowed from that upon the child and others

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/54)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/54)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَعْطَى أُمَّهُ عَطِيَّةً فَمَاتَتْ وَ كَانَتْ قَدْ قَبَضَتِ الَّذِي أَعْطَاهَا وَ بَانَتْ بِهِ قَالَ هُوَ وَ الْوَرَثَةُ فِيهَا سَوَاءٌ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,

‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who gave his mother a gift. So she died and she had taken possession of that which he had given her and was irrevocably freed from it. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Him and the other inheritors are equal with regards to it’.
en.sarwar: 16. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu as from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah who has narrated the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who gives a gift to his mother and she dies. She had taken the gift away from him in her possession. He (the Imam) said, ‘He and the other heirs are the same in dealing with that item.’”
